# PDkef

> 100% open source, privacy-first PDF tools that run locally in the browser. No file uploads, no server, no sign up, no paywalls, completely free.

PDkef is a static web application offering a suite of PDF tools (merge, split, remove pages, compress, PDF to image, sign & fill, unlock, protect) that run directly in the user's browser, on desktop or mobile. Because all processing happens locally using WebAssembly/JavaScript, PDF files never leave the user's device.

## Key Features
- **Open Source**: The entire codebase is public under the MIT license - anyone can inspect exactly what runs on their device.
- **Zero Uploads**: All processing is performed client-side using `@cantoo/pdf-lib`. Files are never sent to a server.
- **Completely Free**: No limits on file size, page count, or number of merges. No signups, paywalls, or watermarks.
- **Flexible Sorting**: Reorder files manually via drag-and-drop, or sort them automatically by file name (natural/numeric sorting) or modification date.
- **Page Previews**: Lazy-loads page-1 thumbnails using `pdfjs-dist` to help verify sorting before merging.
- **Offline Capable**: Serves as a Progressive Web App (PWA) with a custom service worker (`public/sw.js`) that caches all code for offline use.

## Architecture & Security
- **Built with**: Astro (static build mode) + Preact (for the client-side interactive island).
- **Security**: Content-Security-Policy (CSP) is strictly configured to prevent execution of unbundled scripts and locks down `connect-src 'self'` to prevent any third-party network requests or telemetry leakage.
- **Dependencies**: Uses MIT/Apache-2.0 open-source libraries only. No trackers, cookies, or analytics.

## Links
- **Homepage**: https://pdkef.vercel.app/
- **Source Code**: https://github.com/shlomsh/pdkef
- **Developer**: [Shlomi Shemesh](https://github.com/shlomsh)
